Now I can see what happened:
- The blue cross (nodes 16, 19, 20, 21, 22) moved UP by 3 pixels (from row 45 to 42). So UP moves the blue cross up.
- The purple bar (node 23) also changed - it shrunk from 63 to 62 pixels. This is the timer/progress bar.
So the blue cross is the player-controlled object. UP moves it up. The goal is probably to collect squares of the matching color (blue squares). Let me try to move the blue cross to collect the blue squares.
Blue cross center was at (45, 36). After UP, it's at (42, 36).
Blue-centered squares are at:
- Node 7: rows 15-17, cols 47-49
- Node 12: rows 23-25, cols 39-41
- Node 13: rows 23-25, cols 52-54
- Node 17: rows 34-36, cols 47-49
Wait, the squares have blue centers. The blue cross needs to collect the blue squares. Let me think about this...
Actually, looking at the layout:
- Yellow cross is at upper-left area (around row 27, col 21)
- Blue cross is at lower-right (around row 45, col 36)
- Yellow squares: nodes 1,3,3,4,8 (upper area)
- Blue squares: nodes 7,12,13,17 (right area)
The game might be: move your cross to collect squares of your cross's color. The blue cross collects blue squares. Let me try moving the blue cross to collect the blue squares.
Blue cross center is at row 42, col 36). The blue squares are at:
- Node 7: rows 15-17, cols 47-49 (upper right)
- Node 12: rows 23-25, cols 39-41 (middle)
- Node 13: rows 23-25, cols 52-54 (right)
- Node 17: rows 34-36, cols 47-49 (lower right)
The blue cross is at (42, 36). Let me try moving RIGHT to get closer to node 12 (at col 39-41).
Actually, let me try moving RIGHT first to see if it moves right.
